data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: Predicts the time it takes for users to make decisions based on the number of choices available.
data.technical_spec: Google minimizes search decision time by providing relevant results and anticipated suggestions.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: Limits the number of objects an average person can hold in working memory.
data.technical_spec: Search snippet summaries are typically concise, aiding user retention.

data.title: Fitt’s Law
data.description: Time required to rapidly move to a target area is a function of distance to and size of the target.
data.technical_spec: Interactive elements like buttons are designed with optimal size for faster interactions.

data.title: Jakob’s Law
data.description: Users spend most time on other sites, so they prefer your site to work similarly to all other sites.
data.technical_spec: Google search interface follows familiar web conventions for seamless user experience.

data.title: Aesthetic Usability Effect
data.description: Users often perceive aesthetically pleasing design as more usable.
data.technical_spec: Minimalist, visually appealing design contributes to user perception of efficiency.
